ogTitle: Cheero Point vs Cherry Tree Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cheero Point and Cherry Tree Hill.
heading: Cheero Point vs Cherry Tree Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cheero Point, New South Wales and Cherry Tree Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cheero Point has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Cherry Tree Hill (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cheero Point or Cherry Tree Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cheero Point has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Cherry Tree Hill scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cheero Point scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Cherry Tree Hill scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cheero Point or Cherry Tree Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cheero Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
